NousResearch/hermes-agent.summary: Hermes Agent is a self-improving AI agent designed to maintain persistent memory and evolve its capabilities through a built-in learning loop. It autonomously creates and refines skills from experience while utilizing FTS5 session search and dialectic user modeling to recall information across sessions. The system supports a wide range of LLM providers and can be deployed on diverse infrastructure, from low-cost VPS to serverless environments. Users can interact with the agent via a dedicated terminal interface or through multiple messaging platforms via a single gateway process.
highlights: Autonomous skill creation and self-improvement through a closed learning loop
Deployment across six backends including Docker, SSH, and serverless Modal
Multi-platform access via Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, and Signal

NousResearch/Hermes-Function-Calling.summary: Hermes-Function-Calling provides a framework for the Hermes Pro LLM to execute function calling and structured data generation based on provided schemas. It utilizes a recursive loop to generate tool calls, execute them via Python scripts, and return the results to the model for final response generation. The project includes implementations for retrieving financial data via the yfinance library and supports JSON mode for validating outputs against Pydantic models. It employs the ChatML prompt format to ensure compatibility with OpenAI-style multi-turn dialogues.
highlights: Executes function calls for stock prices and financial fundamentals.
Supports JSON mode with Pydantic model validation.
Uses ChatML format for structured multi-turn chat dialogues.

- "Uses ChatML format for structured multi-turn chat dialogues." (The README mentions `chatml` as a default template, but it does not explicitly state it is used for "structured multi-turn chat dialogues.")

NousResearch/atropos.summary: Atropos is an environment microservice framework designed to facilitate asynchronous reinforcement learning for large language models. It utilizes a trajectory API that allows environment services to send data and trainers to pull batches for optimization. The framework supports a wide range of interactive settings, including code execution, multimodal tasks, and multi-turn tool calling. This architecture enables the training of specialized models for domains such as financial prediction and complex function calling.
highlights: Supports dataset, online, multimodal, and code execution environments
Provides a trajectory API for data collection and distribution
Enables RL training for multi-turn tool calling and RLAIF

NousResearch/hermes-agent-self-evolution.summary: Hermes Agent Self-Evolution is an automated optimization framework designed to improve the performance of Hermes Agent without requiring GPU training. It utilizes DSPy and Genetic-Pareto Prompt Evolution (GEPA) to analyze execution traces and mutate skill files, tool descriptions, and system prompts based on reflective feedback. The system validates candidate variants through constraint gates, including size limits and full test suite passes, before proposing improvements via pull requests. It supports evaluation using both synthetic data and real session history from tools like Claude Code and Copilot.
highlights: Optimizes agent skills and prompts using DSPy and GEPA engines
Operates via API calls without requiring local GPU resources
Enforces strict guardrails including size limits and 100% test pass rates

It works by spawning the Hermes CLI in single-query mode, parsing raw stdout into structured transcript objects, and managing session state across heartbeats. The adapter converts ASCII formatting into GFM markdown and supports a wide range of inference providers and toolsets. This allows Hermes to handle assigned issues and respond to comments while maintaining persistent memory and skill integration.
highlights: Supports 8 inference providers and 30+ native tools
Parses raw output into structured GFM markdown and tool cards
Integrates Paperclip-managed and Hermes-native skills via sync APIs

NousResearch/autonovel.summary: Autonovel is an autonomous agent pipeline designed to transform a seed concept into a complete, publishable novel. It utilizes a multi-phase workflow that includes world-building, sequential drafting, and an adversarial revision loop involving dual-persona LLM reviewers. The system integrates mechanical slop detection and voice fingerprinting to ensure prose quality and narrative consistency across tens of thousands of words. Beyond text generation, the project automates typesetting in LaTeX, cover art creation via fal.ai, and multi-voice audiobook production using ElevenLabs.
highlights: Autonomous four-phase pipeline from world-building to final export
Adversarial revision loop with mechanical and LLM-based evaluation
Automated typesetting, cover illustration, and multi-voice audiobook generation

highlights: Documentation for the Hermes AI email agent
Deployment patterns for system setup
Implementation recipes for agent configuration

- "Implementation recipes for agent configuration"

nativ3ai/hermes-agent-camel.summary: This project is a self-improving AI agent designed for autonomous execution with integrated safety boundaries. It utilizes a closed learning loop to create and refine skills from experience while employing a CaMeL-style runtime guard to prevent indirect prompt injections. The agent supports a wide array of LLM providers and can be deployed across various environments, including serverless infrastructure and cloud VMs. Users can interact with the agent via a dedicated terminal interface or through messaging platforms like Telegram, Discord, and Slack.
highlights: Self-improving learning loop for autonomous skill creation and refinement
CaMeL-style runtime guard to block unauthorized sensitive tool side effects
Multi-platform access via CLI and various messaging gateway integrations

kaminocorp/hermes-alpha.summary: Hermes Alpha is an experimental framework designed to test if a stock AI agent can bootstrap an autonomous bug bounty system from first principles. It utilizes a two-agent loop where a persistent Overseer agent manages the codebase, deployment, and optimization of an ephemeral Hunter agent tasked with finding software vulnerabilities. The system operates via a FastAPI-powered web terminal and leverages Elephantasm for memory streams to drive a self-improvement cycle. This approach tests whether an agent can evolve its own skills and tools without pre-built scaffolding.
highlights: Self-improving loop where Overseer evolves Hunter's code and strategy
Web terminal interface using FastAPI, WebSockets, and xterm.js
Deployment on Fly.io with Elephantasm memory and messaging sidecars

beardthelion/hermes-skill-distillation.summary: This project provides a skill distillation pipeline designed to fine-tune Hermes 4 models using real-world agentic trajectories. It captures tool-use sessions from a diverse 30-task battery and converts them into SFT-ready data or live RL signals for the Atropos training framework. The system uses a multi-dimensional reward function to score trajectories based on task completion, efficiency, and error recovery. This approach enables a closed learning loop where production-style agent interactions serve as the primary training data for future model iterations.
highlights: Generates SFT-ready JSONL data from real-world tool usage trajectories
Integrates with Atropos for live reinforcement learning via GRPO
Automated scoring based on completion, efficiency, and error recovery

xaspx/hermes-control-interface.summary: Hermes Control Interface is a self-hosted web dashboard designed to centralize the management of the Hermes AI agent stack. It utilizes a Node.js and Express backend with a Vanilla JS frontend to provide real-time control over agents, files, and system metrics. The interface includes a specialized swarm monitor for tracking agent health and task pipelines via a kanban system. It also integrates a full control plane for managing MCP servers and tracking token usage and costs.
highlights: ZOO Swarm Monitor with kanban pipeline and live event feed
Full MCP server control plane with config editor and log tailing
Real-time chat with streaming, multi-profile support, and tool call cards

ksimback/hermes-ecosystem.summary: Hermes Atlas is a community-curated directory and ecosystem map for the Hermes Agent by Nous Research. It aggregates over 80 quality-filtered repositories, including skills, plugins, and deployment templates, using a vanilla JavaScript frontend and Vercel serverless functions. The platform features a RAG-powered chatbot that answers technical questions grounded in 27 research files using hybrid retrieval and OpenRouter LLMs. Users can search and filter the ecosystem while viewing live GitHub star counts and growth trends cached via Redis.
highlights: Directory of 80+ security-reviewed Hermes Agent tools and integrations
RAG chatbot providing grounded answers from 27 research files
Live GitHub star tracking with Redis-cached sparklines and trends

dodo-reach/hermes-desktop.summary: Hermes Desktop is a native macOS application that provides a dedicated workbench for interacting with Hermes Agent. It connects directly to the Hermes host via SSH to ensure the remote machine remains the sole source of truth without requiring a local mirror or gateway API. The app integrates sessions, Kanban boards, cron jobs, and file editing into a single window, including an embedded terminal for direct shell access. It supports multiple Hermes profiles for multi-agent workflows and is compatible with various hosts, including VPS, Raspberry Pi, and local machines.
highlights: Direct SSH connection without browser wrappers or daemon requirements
Integrated views for sessions, Kanban, skills, and cron jobs
Support for multiple Hermes profiles and universal macOS architecture

clawshell/clawshell.summary: ClawShell is a security-privileged process that acts as a safety harness for the Hermes Agent ecosystem by protecting sensitive credentials and PII. It functions as a proxy between the agent and upstream LLM providers, swapping virtual keys for real ones stored in a restricted directory to ensure the agent never has direct access to credentials. The system performs Data Loss Prevention (DLP) scanning to redact or block sensitive information in request and response bodies. Additionally, it provides secure email isolation through IMAP sender filtering and supports OAuth-based authentication for providers like OpenAI.
highlights: Maps virtual keys to real credentials stored in protected Unix directories
Scans and redacts PII in request and response bodies via regex
Enforces sender-based email filtering for secure mailbox access

nesquena/hermes-webui.summary: Hermes WebUI is a lightweight browser-based interface for the Hermes Agent that provides a graphical alternative to the terminal. It is built using Python and vanilla JavaScript without the need for a build step or external frameworks. The interface features a three-panel layout for managing sessions, chat, and workspace files with full parity to the CLI experience. Users can securely access their existing agent and models via an SSH tunnel or Docker deployment.
highlights: Full feature parity with the Hermes CLI experience
Three-panel layout with workspace file browsing and inline previews
Lightweight architecture using Python and vanilla JavaScript

garrytan/gbrain.summary: GBrain is an opinionated memory and synthesis layer designed to provide AI agents with persistent context across people, companies, and ideas. It combines a self-wiring knowledge graph that extracts entity relationships without LLM calls with a synthesis layer that provides cited answers and gap analysis. The system runs a background daemon to ingest, enrich, and consolidate data from sources like meetings and emails. It integrates directly with Hermes and OpenClaw agents and supports MCP for tools like Claude Code.
highlights: Self-wiring knowledge graph extracts typed entity relationships automatically
Synthesis layer provides cited prose answers with explicit gap analysis
Supports multi-user company brain mode with scoped data access

pyrate-llama/hermes-ui.summary: Hermes UI is a glassmorphic web dashboard designed to serve as the central command center for self-hosted Hermes Agents. It is built as a single-file React 18 application that communicates with the agent through a lightweight Python proxy server. The interface enables users to manage chat sessions, browse workspace files, monitor real-time logs, and schedule cron jobs. It also provides dedicated views for inspecting memory, managing installed skills, and interacting with connected MCP tools.
highlights: Single-file React 18 application with a lightweight Python proxy
Real-time log streaming and live workspace file browser
Integrated management for skills, cron jobs, and MCP tools

kevinmarmstrong/hermes-claude-code-rc.summary: This Hermes Agent plugin enables remote management of Claude Code sessions through a Telegram interface. It bridges the gap between mobile devices and local filesystems by allowing users to initiate authenticated remote control sessions on their host machine via slash commands. The plugin captures the resulting session URL and returns it to the user, enabling full access to local tools and databases from the Claude mobile app. Beyond session management, it supports headless command execution and direct shell access with built-in safety filters for dangerous operations.
highlights: Starts Claude Code remote control sessions via Telegram slash commands
Provides mobile access to local filesystems and MCP servers
Executes headless commands and shell operations on the host machine

AaronWong1999/hermesclaw.summary: HermesClaw is an integration bridge that allows Hermes Agent, OpenClaw, and OpenCode to operate simultaneously on a single WeChat account. It acts as a central iLink poller and routes messages through local proxy servers to prevent the 403 errors and connection drops caused by multiple gateways competing for the same API token. This architecture enables users to switch between different AI brains or activate multiple agents at once using simple chat commands. It also introduces WeChat support for OpenCode, enabling voice-based coding tasks and access to free models like MiniMax M2.5.
highlights: Runs three AI agents simultaneously on one WeChat account
Prevents iLink token conflicts via local proxy routing
Enables agent switching with /hermes, /openclaw, and /opencode commands

stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm.summary: Hermes-LCM is a context management plugin for Hermes Agent that prevents data loss during conversation compression. It utilizes a hierarchical summary DAG and a SQLite message store to compact older context while maintaining a recoverable record of raw messages. The agent can use specialized tools to drill back into compacted material without flooding the main context window. This approach improves retrieval quality and autonomy by integrating source-aware retrieval directly into the active context engine.
highlights: Hierarchical summary DAG for depth-aware context compaction
SQLite store for persisting raw messages and externalized payloads
Drill-down tools including lcm_grep and lcm_expand_query

Codename-11/hermes-relay.summary: Hermes-Relay is a multi-platform companion suite that enables remote control of a server-deployed Hermes agent from Android and desktop devices. It establishes a secure connection over WSS, allowing the agent to interact with the local host as if it were running natively. The Android app supports screen reading, touch interaction, and voice chat, while the desktop CLI and Windows tray app provide terminal access, file system tools, and clipboard integration. This setup eliminates the need for SSH by routing agent commands and local device actions through a unified relay plugin.
highlights: Native Android app for remote screen control and agent interaction
Desktop CLI and Windows tray app for remote terminal access
Bidirectional file, clipboard, and screenshot sharing over WSS

keepnotes-ai/keep.summary: Keep is a reflective memory system for AI agents that facilitates long-term learning and contextual awareness. It processes diverse inputs—including URLs, codebases, and media—by summarizing, embedding, and indexing them into a searchable graph. The system uses 'edge tags' to create navigable relationships between notes, such as linking authors to their specific contributions. It integrates with various agent frameworks via MCP and provides structured prompts to help agents reflect on past actions and current intentions.
highlights: Semantic search with BM25 full-text and ranked graph traversal
Automated indexing for local directories, URLs, and multimedia files
Edge tags create navigable relationships with automatic inverse links

AMAP-ML/SkillClaw.summary: SkillClaw is a skill management framework that enables AI agents to evolve their capabilities through real-world interactions. It functions by running a background evolution loop that automatically deduplicates, improves, and merges skills across different sessions and devices. The system creates a unified skill library that synchronizes experience between multiple agents and users, ensuring that improvements made by one instance are available to all others. It natively integrates with the Hermes Agent ecosystem and supports various other platforms including OpenClaw, QwenPaw, and OpenAI-compatible APIs.
highlights: Automates skill deduplication and quality improvement through background evolution loops
Unifies skill libraries across multiple agents, devices, and user teams
Natively integrates with Hermes, OpenClaw, and OpenAI-compatible APIs

aivrar/portable-hermes-agent.summary: Portable Hermes Agent is a standalone Windows desktop application that provides a graphical interface for the Hermes Agent framework without requiring system-wide installations or administrative rights. It operates by bundling an embedded Python environment and connecting to local models via LM Studio or cloud providers through an OpenAI-compatible API. The platform features over 100 tools for tasks such as music generation, text-to-speech, and ComfyUI image creation, alongside a workflow engine for multi-step automation. Users can also extend the agent's capabilities at runtime using a built-in dynamic tool maker for API wrapping and Python scripting.
highlights: Zero-install Windows desktop GUI with granular permission controls
Over 100 tools including TTS, MusicGen, and ComfyUI integrations
Dynamic tool maker for creating API wrappers and Python handlers

iamagenius00/hermes-a2a.summary: hermes-a2a is a plugin for Hermes Agent that implements Google's A2A protocol to enable direct peer-to-peer communication between autonomous agents. It works by injecting external messages into an agent's active session via HMAC-signed webhooks, allowing for instant responses without polling or session cloning. This architecture ensures agents maintain a single shared memory with their user while collaborating on tasks like debugging, status reporting, or peer consultation. The system includes built-in security features such as prompt injection filtering, outbound redaction, and independent conversation storage to prevent data loss during context compaction.
highlights: Enables peer-to-peer agent communication without manual user relaying
Injects messages into active sessions to maintain consistent agent memory
Triggers instant agent responses via HMAC-signed webhook architecture

Cranot/super-hermes.summary: Super Hermes is a skill registry for the Hermes Agent that enables models to generate their own analytical frameworks, or prisms, for deep code and document inspection. It works by instructing the agent to write specific thinking instructions before execution, allowing it to derive structural trade-offs and conservation laws rather than just identifying surface-level bugs. The system includes specialized commands like /prism-reflect, which provide a constraint report detailing what the analysis missed to ensure transparency in the agent's cognitive limits.
highlights: Teaches agents to write custom thinking instructions for specific problems
Identifies structural conservation laws and trade-offs in complex codebases
Generates constraint reports detailing analysis blind spots and limitations

EfficientContext/ContextPilot.summary: ContextPilot is a context optimization engine designed to accelerate long-context LLM inference by maximizing KV cache reuse. It functions by maintaining a context index that reorders and deduplicates overlapping context blocks into shared prefixes before they reach the inference server. This approach reduces redundant token computation and prefill latency without compromising model reasoning quality. The project provides native integrations for Hermes Agent, OpenClaw, vLLM, and SGLang, supporting both local deployments and cloud APIs.
highlights: Increases cache hits by 4–12× through prefix reordering and deduplication
Reduces prefill latency by 1.5–3× across RAG and agentic workloads
Native plugin support for Hermes Agent and OpenClaw ecosystems

335234131/agent-browser-mcp.summary: Agent-browser-mcp is a Model Context Protocol server that enables AI agents to interact directly with a user's active Chrome browser session rather than a sandboxed environment. The project allows agents to perform page scanning, execute JavaScript, and issue Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) commands. It also supports physical hardware interaction, including real mouse movements, keyboard inputs, and desktop screenshots.
highlights: Operates real Chrome sessions with preserved login states and cookies

jwangkun/hermes-agent-guide.summary: The Hermes Agent White Paper is a comprehensive Chinese-language technical guide designed to bridge the documentation gap for the Hermes Agent framework. highlights: 16-book systematic guide with over 300,000 words of technical content
Deep analysis of five-layer architecture and three-tier memory systems

yepyhun/Brainstack.summary: Brainstack is a local-first memory kernel designed to replace flat memory blobs in Hermes agents with a structured, multi-layered memory model. The system includes a dedicated installer and diagnostic tools to maintain stateful conversation continuity within the Hermes-Agent runtime.

supermemoryai/supermemory.summary: Supermemory is a context and memory layer for AI agents that prevents information loss between conversations. Sahil-SS9/MrHermagi-tutorbot.summary: MrHermagi-tutorbot is a Discord-based AI educational assistant that automates the delivery of structured learning paths using Hermes Agent profiles. The bot utilizes YAML-defined curricula to generate daily lessons consisting of Discord summaries, dark-mode HTML deep-dives, and TTS audio for mobile listening. It features a Socratic teacher persona that delivers content to forum channels and provides interactive Q&A support in dedicated text channels. Users can swap subjects by updating a configuration file without modifying the underlying infrastructure or restarting the gateway.
highlights: Delivers daily lessons via Discord forum threads with TTS audio
Uses YAML files to define and easily swap custom curricula
Provides interactive Q&A using a patient, analogy-driven Socratic persona

Sahil-SS9/hermes-Custom-CLI-Themes.summary: Hermes Custom CLI Themes is a collection of visual skins designed to personalize the Hermes Agent terminal interface. Each theme is defined in a single YAML file that configures color palettes, spinners, branding, and ASCII banner art. Users can apply these skins by placing them in a specific local directory and updating their configuration or using an in-app command. The project includes a variety of themed presets ranging from tactical and sci-fi aesthetics to minimalist investigator styles.
highlights: Provides YAML-based skins for terminal palettes and ASCII branding.
Includes a one-liner script for automated skin installation.
Supports custom extended keys for advanced UI element styling.

mag3nt-com/openclaw-skill.summary: mag3nt-pay is an agent payment skill that enables AI agents to autonomously pay for APIs that return HTTP 402 Payment Required errors. It works by detecting payment protocols from response headers, decoding the payment challenge, and settling the transaction using USDC via Mag3nt virtual cards. The skill supports Base, Solana, and most EVM networks, allowing agents to handle micropayments and subscriptions without human intervention. It is compatible with OpenClaw, Hermes Agent, and any framework following the agentskills.io standard.
highlights: Automates payments for HTTP 402 responses using USDC
Supports Pay Link, x402, MPP, and AP2 protocols
Compatible with OpenClaw, Hermes Agent, and agentskills.io standard
